title: Inside Sales Executive
description: **Inside Sales Executive** My client, a long established and trusted supplier of industrial products to Local Authorities, Merchants and Contractors is looking to add an **Inside Sales Executive** to their team to help them capitalise on current market opportunities and grow their customer base and revenues. * Location: Dublin 22 – The role will initially be fully onsite but thereafter some level of remote working is possible * Salary: €33000\-38000 depending upon experience \+ the ability to earn circa 20\-40% in additional on target earnings * Full Time staff opportunity **The Opportunity:** This is an excellent opportunity to join a well\-established company with a strong client base, including many of the countries Local Authorities that is looking to significantly grow its business. You will join the team in a sales focused role and have a real opportunity to develop your career in this area while also having a strong additional earnings potential on top of your fixed salary. **Main Responsibilities:** * Develop strong and leverageable relationships with existing customers. * Identify sales opportunities for the full range of products to existing customers. * Develop routes to market for new products by creative promotional activities. * Understand the different approaches to growing sales to customers including Local Authorities, Merchants, Contractors and Industry. * Use all available marketing/merchandising tools to promote the full range of products to all available customers. * Manage customer accounts and ensure all customers are afforded the highest levels of customers service. * Understand and keep aware of product stock levels to ensure rapid response to customer need. * Support the field sales operation and supply leads as appropriate. * Ensure margins are maintained on all sales. * Achieve personal and divisional targets. **Required Skills and Experience:** *Essential:* * You should have at least 1 years’ experience in an inside sales role focused on Business to Business sales. * A bachelor’s degree level qualification. * A proven ability to achieve a sales target in a Business to Business role. * The ability to manage existing customer accounts while also targeting and cold calling potential new customers. * Exceptional organisational skills and strong skills with tools such as sales databases and Excel. * First class communication skills with both internal team members and external stakeholders such as customers and suppliers. * A self\-driven and target orientated personality. *Nice to have:* * An understanding of industrial products such as Water Meters and Piping gained via education or within a working environment. * An engineering or related bachelor’s degree level qualification. * Experience selling into local authorities and contractors. **Who would this role suit:** This role would suit a number of profiles such as a recent graduate with 1 years’ experience of Business to Business sales who is looking for an exciting new opportunity with strong earning potential and the chance to sell an industrial type of product. If you are self\-motivated, comfortable selling into existing customers while also prospecting new customers this could be the right opportunity for you. It may also suit an experienced professional from an industrial products company who understands products such as Water Meters and Piping and who has worked with Local Authorities, Contractors and Merchants and who is looking to move into a sales roles. **What is on offer:** * An excellent opportunity to develop and grow your career in a friendly and supportive environment. * A competitive fixed salary and benefits package with the opportunity to earn an attractive additional on target earnings package. * The chance to work remotely in the future and once you have become comfortable and established in your role. You should expect the for the first 6 months at least you will work mostly onsite. *Apply in total confidence today* *Horizon Blue Talent Consulting is a Talent Acquisition Consulting business operating on behalf of a client in relation to this vacancy. By submitting your application for this vacancy you are consenting to the Privacy Policy of Horizon Blue Talent Consulting which can be accessed via this link http://www.horizonbluetc.com/\#privacy\-policy* *Horizon Blue Talent Consulting takes the privacy of your data very seriously and will only use it for the purposes for which you have engaged Horizon Blue Talent Consulting. Should you wish to have your data removed at any time please use the following link http://www.horizonbluetc.com/\#contact\-us*

title: Integrations Specialist
description: This is an opportunity to play a key role in shaping and delivering critical digital healthcare systems, working at the heart of a growing **EPIC application environment** . As an **HL7 Integration Specialist** , you’ll act as the bridge between **clinical users and technology** , designing and delivering robust integration solutions that enable seamless data flow across complex healthcare ecosystems. We’re looking for someone who combines **deep technical expertise** with the ability to **lead, influence, and collaborate \-** someone who can both deliver hands\-on and guide others. **Location:** Ireland **Working Model:** Flexible hybrid working What You’ll Be Doing * Design, build, test, and support **HL7, FHIR, API, and file\-based integrations** across EHR, ERP, cloud, and third\-party systems * Act as a **trusted advisor** on integration strategy, architecture, and interoperability standards * Lead and support **Epic EMR implementations** , including interfaces, upgrades, and post go\-live optimisation * Translate complex business and clinical requirements into **clear technical solutions** * Take ownership of **complex integration issues** , acting as the key escalation point * Develop and maintain **standards, processes, and documentation** to support scalable delivery * Operate as a **player\-coach** , balancing hands\-on delivery with mentoring and supporting team members * Collaborate with stakeholders across clinical, operational, and technical teams to drive innovation and improvement What You’ll Bring **Essential:** * Strong experience with **HL7 (v2, FHIR, CDA), APIs, and healthcare data exchange** * Hands\-on experience with **integration engines** (e.g. InterSystems, Rhapsody, Cloverleaf, Mirth, BizTalk, Corepoint, Qvera, IBM Integration Bus) * Experience working with **EPIC** * Solid understanding of **integration design, testing, and deployment practices** * Ability to **mentor and support junior team members** * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work in a **fast\-paced, 24/7 environment** **Desirable:** * Epic Bridges certification * Experience with **programming/scripting** (e.g. C\#, JavaScript, VBScript, .NET) * Knowledge of **SQL and database technologies** (MSSQL, Oracle) * Familiarity with **integration architecture tools** (e.g. Visio) Why This Role? This is more than just an integration role \- it’s an opportunity to: * Work on **high\-impact healthcare systems** that directly improve patient care * Influence **digital transformation at scale** * Grow your career in a **collaborative, forward\-thinking environment** * Take on a role that blends **technical depth with leadership and strategy**

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Programme, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise, while making real contributions to Autodesk’s manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

description: **Internal Audit \& Compliance Analyst** A well\-established, market\-leading organisation operating in a high\-volume, client\-facing environment is seeking an Internal Audit \& Compliance Analyst to join its finance function. This role sits within a busy operational finance team and will play a key part in maintaining a strong control environment, supporting audit processes, and driving continuous improvement across financial workflows and systems. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform daily and monthly control checks across financial processes, ensuring adherence to internal policies and procedures * Support internal audit reviews and assist with external audit requirements * Prepare and deliver regular compliance and control reports for senior management * Work closely with operational teams to ensure processes are understood and consistently applied * Act as a point of contact for queries relating to systems and workflow processes * Support the implementation and enhancement of financial systems and process automation initiatives * Assist in developing and documenting controls, procedures, and training materials * Identify areas for improvement across finance operations and contribute to process efficiencies * Support banking and payment processes, ensuring appropriate controls are in place **About You** * Part\-qualified or recently qualified accountant (ACCA / ACA / CIMA) * 2–4 years’ experience in audit, compliance, or finance operations * Strong understanding of financial controls and processes * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, high\-volume environment * Strong attention to detail with a structured and methodical approach * Confident communicator with the ability to engage with non\-finance stakeholders * Systems\-savvy, with strong Excel skills and exposure to finance or workflow systems

description: **Investment Analyst Capital Markets** **Remuneration is dependant on experience DOE \+ outstanding benefits.** **Company Description** Our client is a leading real estate advisory firm operating across multiple sectors of the Irish market, including Office \& Business Space, Retail, Development Land, Hotels \& Leisure, F\&B, Advisory Services (Valuations, Debt Advisory, Landlord \& Tenant, Rating) and Property Management. title: Junior CAMO Engineer
description: **Reporting Structure** Reports directly to the Fleet Principal Engineer (TS\-S), or by individual appointment to the appointed CAMO Engineer (TS\-E). The TS\-J role exists to support the TS\-S or TS\-E in ensuring all aircraft type\-related continuing airworthiness activities are carried out. Responsibility for workload distribution sits with the TS\-S, while TS\-J remains accountable for the quality of their own duties and for flagging any suspected shortfalls in oversight or resourcing. **Duties and Responsibilities** * Maintain awareness of the airworthiness status of each aircraft in the managed fleet, ensuring all operational and emergency equipment required for flight is serviceable * Liaise with aircraft, engine, and component manufacturers, maintenance organisations, and the Flight Operations Division * Coordinate MEL and PFI activities in line with aircraft ferry and delivery requirements * Assist in the development, amendment, and control of the Aircraft Maintenance Programme * Support airworthiness records management and maintenance data control * Assist in the management of Airworthiness Directives, Service Bulletins, modifications, and repairs * Contribute reports, statistics, and analysis to the Reliability Programme and support evaluation of maintenance programme effectiveness * Support planning of all maintenance activities, including scheduled and unscheduled base maintenance and major asset visits * Support control and monitoring of aircraft, engine, landing gear, life\-limited parts (LLP), and hard\-time (HT) components * Monitor and schedule non\-routine and deferred maintenance activities, including MEL/CDL requirements * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date records in the AMOS IT system * Support the accomplishment of operational directives, airworthiness directives, and all continued airworthiness requirements, including CRMs, LLPs, airworthiness limitations, CDCCL requirements, and approved modifications and repairs * Issue clear and accurate work orders to maintenance contractors * Participate in material planning relevant to the maintenance activity * Carry out any additional duties within the TS\-J functional area as directed by the TS\-S or TS\-E

title: Junior Data Coordinator
description: **Where the role sits** Commercial advice is only as good as the data it sits on. Before we can tell a client where their margin is leaking, where capital is trapped, or whether an acquisition assumption holds, we have to produce a version of their numbers that actually reconciles. Most of the time, client data arrives across multiple systems, in multiple formats, with overlapping definitions and inconsistent totals. The Junior Data Coordinator owns the work of turning that material into a clean, reliable analytical base. **Core responsibilities** * Consolidating client data from accounting exports, CRM systems, pricing files, sales reports, and spreadsheets into unified working datasets * Reconciling the same figure across sources and documenting why the numbers differ * Building standard cuts that analysts can use straight away: realised price by customer, margin by product, contribution by channel, cohort performance, fixed versus variable cost splits * Setting up file structures, naming conventions, and version control so engagement data is always findable * Keeping a transformation log so any number in a client deliverable can be traced back to its source * Preparing data for senior review meetings and adjusting in response to new requests, often under time pressure **What we need from you** **Essential** * Strong spreadsheet skills. You are comfortable with lookups, pivot tables, conditional logic, basic array functions, and keeping large files organised. Excel or Google Sheets, either is fine. * Comfort with structured data. CSVs, exports, and tables do not intimidate you. You can spot a formatting issue, a duplicate row, or a broken total. * Accuracy as a default setting. You check your own work before handing it over. You do not round away problems to finish faster. * Clear written English for documentation, file notes, and internal handovers. * Organised by habit. Your folder structures make sense to other people, not just to you. **Helpful but not required** * Basic SQL. If you do not have it, you will need to pick it up in your first few months, and we will support that. * Exposure to Power Query, Python (pandas), or another data\-cleaning tool * Any experience with accounting software, ERP systems, or CRM exports (Xero, QuickBooks, NetSuite, HubSpot, Salesforce, and similar all count) * A degree or course of study in finance, accounting, economics, data, operations, or a related field * Personal or academic projects involving data cleaning, analysis, or reporting **What we are not looking for** You do not need to call yourself a data analyst. You do not need a portfolio of dashboards. You do not need years of experience. If you have solid spreadsheet fundamentals, a careful working style, and you want to build real data skills on live commercial engagements, we want to hear from you. **What you will get out of the role** * Direct exposure to how commercial decisions are actually built from the data up * A defined route into analyst work for those who prove the fundamentals * Training and feedback from senior advisors, not from a handbook * A team where accurate, methodical work is the thing that gets respected

title: Junior Health & Safety Officer

description: **About Us** Since the 1950s, Leo Lynch has been providing the pharmaceutical, semiconductor, industrial, food, commercial and retail industries with professional project management and high\-calibre craftsmanship. At Leo Lynch, we build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Leo Lynch employs the best people, invests heavily in training and education, and ensures that the highest standards of health, safety and governance are applied throughout the organization. **About The Role** Reporting to the Senior Estimator and as an integral part of the estimating team, you will be responsible for assisting to prepare accurate mechanical cost estimates in a timely manner. You will become proficient in the use of cost estimating software with training provided. You will work on tenders for projects such as Pharmaceuticals, Food \& Beverage, Data Centres, Semi\-Conductor, Commercial, etc across Ireland, UK and Europe. **Responsibilities** You will assist to: * Conduct in\-depth reviews of tender documents, drawings, specifications, schedules, etc * Preform accurate quantity take\-offs and mark up measured drawings * Request, manage and evaluate supplier and sub\-contractor quotations * Issue tender queries and RFIs * Prepare costed bill of quantities * Prepare tender pricing schedules and other tender return documents * Manage data base * Attend meetings and site visits are when required * Liaise with clients, consultants, contractors, and wider project teams * Other related tasks as instructed **Candidate Requirements** * Degree in either Mechanical, Process, Building Services Engineering or other related field * Previous experience in a contracting environment is beneficial but not essential * Strong computer skills especially in the use of MS Office * Understanding of mechanical pipework systems, HVAC, plumbing and other mechanical building services systems * Strong attention to detail * Ability to manage own workload and meet deadlines * Must display excellent time management, communication, numerical, analytical and organisational skills * Problem solving skills with attention to detail and multitasking abilities

title: Junior Purchasing Assistant
description: **Company Overview** Founded in 1998, Doyles Veg Prep is one of Ireland's leading suppliers of fresh fruit and vegetables. Operating a sixday delivery schedule with a fully refrigerated fleet, we supply a diverse customer base including hotels, catering companies, and many of Ireland's bestknown bars and restaurants. Quality, consistency, and reliability are at the core of everything we do. Due to continued growth, we are seeking to recruit a Junior Purchasing Assistant to support our Purchasing Team. **Role Purpose** The Junior Purchasing Assistant will support the buying team with daytoday purchasing activities, supplier coordination, and stock management. This is an entrylevel role suited to someone with strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and a keen interest in the food or fresh produce supply chain, with clear development opportunities into a Buyer role. ***Key Responsibilities*** **Purchasing \& Supply Support** * Assist Buyers with daily purchasing of fresh produce * Raise and amend purchase orders in line with demand forecast and sales requirements * Communicate with suppliers regarding orders, deliveries, shortages, and substitutions * Track supplier confirmations and follow up on late or incomplete deliveries * Support price checks and ensure accurate pricing on ordering systems **Stock \& Inventory Support** * Monitor stock levels and flag low stock, overstocking, or potential waste * Assist with inventory accuracy and stock rotation processes * Support stock reconciliation and investigate variances as required **Quality \& Compliance** * Support quality checks on incoming goods in line with company food safety and quality standards * Report quality issues to Buyers and suppliers and assist with followup actions * Ensure all purchasing processes comply with food safety and internal procedures **Systems \& Administration** * Maintain accurate purchasing records and supplier information * Update internal systems with pricing, product, and supplier data * Support reporting on purchasing activity, pricing, and supplier performance **Team Collaboration** * Work closely with Warehouse, Operations, and Quality teams to ensure alignment between purchasing and delivery requirements * Escalate supply risks, shortages, or issues promptly Contribute to continuous improvement within the purchasing function * ***Key Requirements*** * Strong interest in food, fresh produce, or supply chain operations * Excellent organisational and timemanagement skills * Ability to work effectively in a fastpaced environment * Strong attention to detail, particularly with orders, pricing, and quality * Good numerical skills and confidence working with data * Strong communication and teamwork skills * Proficiency in Excel or similar systems * Proactive, flexible, and eager to learn ***Desirable*** * Experience in retail, wholesale, food production, or FMCG * Knowledge of seasonal produce or food supply chains * Qualification or ongoing study in Business, Supply Chain, Food, or related field ***What We Offer*** * Entry point into a commercial buying and supply chain career * Exposure to fresh produce markets and supplier networks * Support, training, and development opportunities * A dynamic, handson working environment * Potential progression into a Buyer role

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor – Maplegrange (Navan \& Dublin)** **About Us** Maplegrange is a well\-established plastering and construction company, backed by over 30 years of expertise across both commercial and residential projects. With offices in Navan and Dublin, we have built a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality workmanship and reliable service to our clients. Due to our continued growth, we are seeking a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our expanding team. **The Role** This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated individual looking to develop their career in quantity surveying. Working alongside our Senior Quantity Surveyor, you will gain hands\-on experience across a range of commercial and residential projects, supporting the successful delivery of works from start to finish. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the preparation of project budgets, cost plans, and forecasts * Support cost analysis and value engineering exercises * Assist with procurement processes, including obtaining quotations and liaising with subcontractors * Monitor project costs and progress under the guidance of senior team members * Help prepare interim valuations, variations, and final accounts * Maintain accurate records and documentation * Liaise with internal teams, clients, and suppliers as required **Requirements** * Degree (or working towards) in Quantity Surveying or related discipline * Strong attention to detail and good organisational skills * Fluent in English (written and spoken) * Willingness to learn and develop professionally * Flexible and adaptable approach to work * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team environment * Full, clean driving licence **What We Offer** * Competitive salary starting from €45,000 (depending on experience) * Ongoing training and mentorship from experienced professionals * Opportunity to grow and progress within a dynamic and expanding company **Why Join Maplegrange?** This is a very exciting opportunity for the right candidate to begin or further their career with a respected and growing company. Maplegrange offers a supportive environment with excellent opportunities to learn, develop, and progress long\-term. If you are eager to build a career in quantity surveying, we would love to hear from you. Please send your CV to nicola@maplegrange.ie Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person Application deadline: 08/05/2026 Expected start date: 25/05/2026

title: Junior Speaker Producer
description: **About Us** How did Web Summit become, in the words of Forbes, “the best tech conference on the planet”? Meaningful connections. Our tech events are unmissable because we make it easier for the right people to meet and connect. Everyone at Web Summit works towards this goal. And we’re just getting started. We’re always looking to build on the impact we have already made at Web Summit. In the coming years we’ll take Web Summit to new markets, promoting global connectivity, highlighting important issues and connecting global leaders – all while making a positive impact on the environment and communities we encounter. To build a better company, we have to better ourselves. We do that by finding the most ambitious people to work with us. **The Role** Behind every speaker confirmed for Web Summit is a mountain of research, flawless logistics, and relentless follow\-up. As a Junior Speaker Producer, you will be the right hand to our Senior Talent Bookers. This is an apprenticeship in the high\-stakes world of global talent acquisition. You will learn how to identify the next big thing, how to bypass gatekeepers, and how to manage the egos and expectations of the world’s elite. Your goal is to support the team producing big results while absorbing the skills to eventually close your own Tier\-1 deals. **What You Will Achieve** * Target Intelligence: Conduct deep\-dive research on potential speakers, their recent announcements, and their pain points to craft the perfect pitch. * Outreach Support: Draft compelling, high\-level correspondence. You’ll be responsible for the first wave of outreach and keeping the momentum alive in long\-lead threads. * Administrative Excellence: Manage speaker data, track confirmation statuses, and coordinate the complex scheduling requirements of high\-profile offices. * Briefing \& Preparation: Create ‘Speaker Dossiers’ for our team, ensuring that when we get a meeting with a VIP, we know everything about them. * On\-Site Support: During events, you’ll be on the ground, shadowing the Senior Producers and acting as a secondary point of contact for speakers to ensure their experience is world\-class. **Who You Are** * The Ultimate Researcher: You can find the email address or the right Chief of Staff for a recluse billionaire in twenty minutes. You are a digital detective. * Radically Organised: You thrive in the details. While the Senior Producers are out networking, you are ensuring the CRM is updated, the briefs are sharp, and nothing \- absolutely nothing \- falls through the cracks. * Eager \& Scrappy: You don't wait to be told what to do. You see a gap, and you fill it. You have hustle genes, but are currently looking for the mentorship to refine them. * Unflappable: You can handle a fast\-paced environment where priorities shift every hour. You take feedback well and view "not yet" as a challenge, not a defeat. **Skills We Like** * Experience: Fast\-paced environment (PR, talent agency, journalism, events, TV, Radio, or high\-end hospitality). * Communication: Exceptional writing skills. You must be able to sound professional, persuasive, and succinct. * Tech Savvy: Expert at LinkedIn, CRM tools, and finding info in the dark corners of the web. * Curiosity: Willingness to learn, ask questions, and scale skills quickly. * Personality: High Social IQ. You need to be someone people enjoy working with, even under intense pressure. **Benefits And Perks** * Subsidised healthcare, dental, employee assistance programme (EAP) and more. * Pension contribution up to 6%(Irish based only) * A dedicated in\-house L\&D department, with access to workshops, online learning and resources to help you excel in your career development. * Company laptop, generous annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * Our very own Tramway Team, which drives community and morale\-boosting events for employees to participate in. * Annual company get\-togethers, charity days, and monthly wellbeing talks. * Wellness subsidy issued to all employees of €1000 per year **At Web Summit, we embrace diversity and inclusion. We want talent from all walks of life to help us on our journey to digitise the conference sector. Hiring decisions are made purely on experience, skills and the needs of the business.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Junior Supply Chain Planner
description: **Kick\-start your career in supply chain with a growing team where you’ll learn, develop, and make an impact from day one.** We’re looking for a motivated Junior Supply Chain Planner to support our day\-to\-day operations, ensuring orders run smoothly, data stays accurate, and suppliers stay on track. This is a great opportunity for someone early in their career who wants to grow into a full Supply Chain Planner role over time. **What you’ll be doing:** * Raise and send purchase orders to suppliers * Track orders and ensure deliveries arrive on time * Follow up with suppliers for confirmations and updates * Keep planning systems and data accurate and up to date * Review production reports and highlight any issues * Monitor stock levels and investigate discrepancies * Upload shipment and packing documentation * Work closely with warehouse, production, and procurement teams * Support reporting and share updates on supply chain activity * Help identify improvements using data and insights **What we’re looking for:** * Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Engineering, or similar * Experience in a manufacturing or production environment (preferred) * Strong Excel skills (e.g. pivot tables, VLOOKUP) * Familiarity with ERP systems or data tools (e.g. Power BI) is a plus * Knowledge of Lean Six Sigma or procurement is an advantage **Key skills:** * Strong attention to detail and accuracy * Good analytical and problem\-solving ability * Organised, with the ability to manage multiple tasks * Clear communicator and team player * Fluent English (written and spoken) **Benefits:** * Performance \- KPI bonus * Yearly bonus * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Employee Assistance Program * Additional annual leave from 1 year service Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €35,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Naas, County Kildare: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: KYC AML Analyst (12-Month FTC)
description: Who we are: TrueLayer is Europe’s fastest\-growing Pay by Bank network. We power smarter, safer and faster payments by combining real\-time bank payments with financial and identity data. E\-commerce, iGaming and Financial Services businesses use our products to onboard new users, accept money and make payouts in seconds, and at scale. Live across 22 countries and with 1 new user joining TrueLayer’s Pay by Bank network every 3 seconds, we’re in a very exciting growth stage. This is why we’re expanding the team across our offices in London, Milan, and Dublin. Listed in Sifted as one of the 100 fastest growing start\-ups in UK \& Ireland, and CNBC’s World Top Fintech Companies, we’re trusted by industry leaders like RyanAir and Just Eat Takeaway for continued innovation within our payment products. To date, we’ve raised over $300 million from world\-renowned investors including Stripe, Tiger Global, Northzone, and Tencent. But this is just the beginning. We’re looking for people who are excited about joining the Pay by Bank revolution and helping us change the way the world pays. Could this be you? **Description** TrueLayer Underwriting Team is an integral part of our Commercial function, focusing on enabling seamless client experiences during the onboarding journey. This team specialises in Due Diligence/KYC onboarding reviews and AML investigations, contributing to a smooth and compliant onboarding process for our clients. This is a meaningful opportunity for a Due Diligence/KYC Specialist to join TrueLayer in enhancing the financial crime element of the client onboarding process. As a KYC/AML Analyst, you will maintain our first line of defence against money laundering and financial terrorism while supporting the rapid growth of our business. Your work will protect TrueLayer from financial crime exposure, ensuring that we enter and maintain business relationships that align with our compliance obligations and ethical standards. Sitting within a client\-facing team under the Commercial function, this role provides valuable insight into our customer base and the products available to them. We are looking for a Due Diligence/KYC subject matter expert who is well\-versed in financial crime, with a clear focus on KYC and due diligence processes. **What we expect from you as a KYC/AML Analyst:** * Conduct KYB and KYC checks on prospective customers. * Complete Due Diligence on high\-risk and complex customer use cases at onboarding to assess and manage the financial crime risks of new business relationships. * Identify and communicate financial crime risks for prospective customers, ensuring that the customer risk profile falls within TrueLayer’s risk appetite. * Perform Customer Screening, including PEP, Sanction and adverse media checks. * Perform on\-going monitoring on customers and associated individuals \- working PEP and sanctions alerts and ensuring rationale and outcomes are recorded. * Conduct periodic reviews and event triggered reviews where required. * Complete peer reviews on onboarding and ongoing DD review cases. * Conduct agent onboarding and monitoring of agents on an ongoing basis. * Act as a subject matter expert and escalation point for financial crime issues. * Contribute to the ongoing improvement of systems and processes. * Be comfortable working to/ setting KPIs and support in the accurate collation of MI for Senior Management Team/ external reporting. **Requirements** **About you** * Experience conducting due diligence (DD) reviews on corporate customers including conducting KYC checks, customer screening (PEP and sanctions checks) and assessing financial crime risks. * Experience dealing with complex business models and a range of different business customer types. * Good working knowledge of Financial Crime risks, AML controls and KYC/CDD best practices. * Ability to act as an SME for 1LOD DD, supporting other business areas with financial crime matters. * Comfortable working with other functions and questioning or escalating, where appropriate. * Attention to detail, with an interest in learning and engaging with others. * Experience in optimising due diligence processes and procedures. **We would be particularly interested if you have:** * Having experience onboarding eCommerce merchants is valued, as well as with iGaming/gambling or cryptocurrency businesses. * Experience in Finance, a Financial Technology company or a technology company; * Experience in a progressive and innovative technology startup. * Experience in working with various KYC and authentication software providers. **Don’t meet all the requirements?** Multiple studies have shown that women and people of underrepresented groups are sometimes less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single requirement. At Truelayer, we are committed to building a diverse workforce, so if you are excited about this role and have the essential skills, but not the entire checklist \- we’d still love to hear from you! **Benefits** **What you can expect from us:** * Flexible hours and hybrid working. Split your time 50/50 between working from home and our welcoming offices in Dublin 🇮🇪 spending 2\-3 days per week in the office. * Need to collect the kids from childcare? Love a workout in the gym first thing? No worries, we trust you to do your best work within our hybrid framework 🧘 * Prorated \- 24 days holiday as standard ✈️ with flexible bank holidays, so you can take those days whenever you like 🌍 * Prorated \- 12 fully\-paid wellbeing days a year and your birthday off (on top of the holiday allowance) 🕊️ * Life Cover as part of our benefits package, offering financial protection and peace of mind for you and your loved ones 🛟 * Prorated \- Generous parental leave, above and beyond statutory requirements 👪 * Competitive pension contribution at 4% \& 4% 🧓 * Private health insurance from the day you start 🧑‍⚕️ * Membership of mental wellbeing platform Spill and premium Calm subscription * Prorated \- A €1000 budget to spend on learning \& development each year 📚 * Free lunch from Just Eat 🥙 up to 3 days a week when you are in the office At TrueLayer, we don’t just do inclusion and diversity. We embrace people that have different opinions, perspectives and personalities. Because we believe that by seeing the world from all sorts of angles, we can make life better for all the people who live in it. We strongly encourage applications from underrepresented groups (e.g. people of colour, the LGBTQ\+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from all socio\-economic backgrounds). If you’d like to discuss alternative working patterns, please let us know. We will always aim to make appropriate adjustments to ensure we are fully inclusive to people with different needs during our interview process. So if you need us to make any adjustments to suit your individual needs please let us know \- we’ll be happy to support you.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Maintenance Craft - Fitter

Cavan – onsite role **Tirlán** Tirlán, is a world\-class food and nutrition co\-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands. Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition. **Role Profile** We have an opportunity for a Maintenance Fitter to join Tirlán at our plant in Virginia Co Cavan. title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. We are now looking for a **Maintenance Technician** (Permanent role) to join our team in Dundalk, County Louth. This is a **Night Shift** role, covering Thursday to Saturday 12 hours shift (7pm to 7am). **Summary Of Role** * Responsible for ensuring machine change over’s, stoppages and breakdowns are undertaken in a timely manner to targets and with minimum disruption to production schedules. * Undertake planned routine maintenance and maintain schedules ensuring that all equipment and plant are able to operate safely, efficiently, and in compliance with GMP practices. * Fault find to an expert level on Electrical and Mechanical systems. * Multiskilled able to program PLC’s and to participate in technically involved projects to improve or create new processes. * Mechanically able to design systems and develop to implementation. * Implement continuous improvements on equipment and carry out to a high standard within GMP guidelines. * Create MOC’s and manage through the QMS. * Create design of experiments and use other fault\-finding techniques to improve processes. **Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Ensure that maintenance areas and equipment are kept in a clean, tidy and secure state (including storage areas). * Adherence and recording of defined routine maintenance schedules; covering all equipment and plant. * Rapid and flexible attention to all breakdowns/stoppages to minimise loss of production, liaising with Engineering Supervisor and Operational Engineering Manager. * Carry out/Participate in A3 Problem solving activities on major issues and lead to Root cause. * Analysis of equipment redesign of any systems/equipment to improve process. * Planning and implementation of engineering improvement projects, designing and developing to Validation. * Advise Engineering Supervisor and Operational Engineering Manager of any machine or plant which requires major repair or maintenance. * Highlight potential areas for improvement within Production area regarding equipment or procedural changes. * Liaise with Production on identifying areas for continuous improvement and implement changes using correct change procedure. * Strip down, cleaning and reassembling of production equipment as per routine maintenance schedules. * Perform machine set\-ups and changeovers to target times as required in line with production requirements. * To actively assist in looking at ways to improve changeover times by procedure and method. * Ensure the timely and accurate completion of all documentation and paperwork such as tooling logs, setting sheets, online working sheets, PPM sheets. * Improve and maintain the levels of current Good Manufacturing Practice. * Assist with the training of colleagues as required. * The use of workshop equipment to manufacture piece parts and repair as required. * Assist with projects on trials, and new product introductions as required. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company. **Essential** **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience:** * Advanced Certificate (Craft\- level 6 NFQ) in a mechanical or electrical discipline. Bachelor’s or Associate Degree or Diploma in Engineering / Certificate in Engineering. The successful candidate should display a dynamic and proactive approach to manage problems. * Self\-motivated with a results driven approach. * Flexibility to work across different shifts on request in line with business needs. * Adaptable and ability to work collaboratively. * Proficient in Microsoft Office products (incl. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Take care of your own health and safety and that of people who may be affected by what you do (or do not do). * Co\-operate with others on health and safety, and not interfere with, or misuse, anything provided for your health, safety, or welfare. **Desirable** * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients.
