title: Specialist, (Supervisory), Fund/Client Accounting Representative II
description: At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators and champions of inclusion, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of **Specialist (Supervisory) to join our Fund Accounting team.** This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. **In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:*** Complete allocation of duties to ensure accurate and timely delivery of daily NAV calculations in a fast paced and rapidly evolving environment * Review the work completed by more junior team members and act as point of contact for queries * Maintain open lines of communication with key internal teams to ensure accurate delivery of NAV critical inputs, in line with internal service delivery agreements * Investigate and ensure full closure of operational and audit queries, from both internal and external stakeholders * Train, coach and guide other team members as required to ensure knowledge sharing across the team * Ensure escalation of items requiring urgent resolution to Management Team in a timely manner * Participating in month end requirements and year end deliverables **To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:*** 3\-6 ;years’ Fund Accounting experience within Operations, with 3\-4 years’ experience using Enterprise Invest One * Deep knowledge of the components of daily NAV calculations and complex financial instruments * Skilled in critically analysing all aspects of NAV Production to identify items requiring further investigation or escalation * Knowledge of Fund Accounting requirements for UK OEICs and/or Luxembourg SICAVs an advantage * Third level degree qualified, ideally in Accounting or Business * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Proven track record at being attentive to detail and well organised * Motivated to acquire knowledge, develop and advance * Ability to be flexible and work effectively both within a team structure and independently **At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at:** BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn **Here’s a few of our recent awards:*** America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 * “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 **Our Benefits and Rewards:** BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. title: Sr DevOps Engineer
description: **Role: Sr DevOps Engineer** **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Duration:** **12\+ Months Contract** **What you will do** · Design, build, and enhance CI/CD pipelines that enable fast, reliable deployment of microservices across multiple regions. · Support and evolve multi\-tenant and multi\-region deployment models as we transition to a modern environment strategy. · Build and maintain Kubernetes\-based platforms, delivering secure, scalable,and developer\-friendly environments. · Implement Infrastructure\-as\-Code using Terraform and Helm to standardise and automate infrastructure provisioning. · Strengthen observability, monitoring, and alerting to proactively identify issues and support high\-quality delivery. · Improve developer experience through automation, self\-service tooling, reusable patterns, and scalable pipelines. · Embed security and compliance into delivery workflows, ensuring strong auditability and policy adherence. · Collaborate with engineering to troubleshoot and optimise deployment processes and runtime environments. · Champion modern DevOps and platform engineering practices throughout the engineering organisation. **What you will need to have** · Proven experience in DevOps or platform engineering roles supporting distributed, cloud\-based systems. · Strong hands\-on experience with AWS cloud services and cloud\-native architectures. · Experience with Kubernetes, container orchestration, and service deployment patterns. · Expertise in Terraform and Helm, with a strong automation mindset. · Hands\-on experience building and operating CI/CD pipelines — ideally Jenkins. · Strong Docker, containerisation, and automation fundamentals. · Familiarity with modern observability tooling and best practices. · A focus on creating scalable, repeatable, and secure delivery workflows. · Passion for enabling development teams and improving tooling, velocity, and reliability. **Nice to have** · Experience working in financial services or other regulated environments. · Exposure to multi\-region and multi\-tenant platform strategies. · Experience supporting systems in an event\-driven environment (Kafka familiarity). · Background in legacy\-to\-modern migration initiatives. · Familiarity with GitOps tooling or patterns. · Knowledge of compliance automation and security policy enforcement in pipelines.

title: Sr. Engineer, DevOps
description: **Job Overview** We are seeking an experienced Sr. Engineer, DevOps to play a key role in ensuring the stability, scalability, and automation of our CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure, and deployment processes. This position combines deep technical expertise with a strong focus on best practices, driving improvements across our global SaaS platform. The Senior Engineer will collaborate closely with development and operations teams to optimize workflows, enhance reliability, and implement cutting\-edge DevOps solutions. The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of software development principles and exceptional troubleshooting and debugging skills to resolve complex issues efficiently. Off hours support as needed. This is a hybrid position based at Windmill Lane, Dublin 2, our strategic hub for AI development in Ireland. **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs. **Responsibilities** **Mentorship \& Technical Leadership:** * Provide guidance and mentorship to junior DevOps engineers, helping them grow technical skills and adopt best practices. * Share expertise in CI/CD, infrastructure automation, and troubleshooting to elevate team capabilities. * Support collaborative problem\-solving and knowledge sharing across Ireland, India, and North America time zones. * Promote alignment on technical standards and encourage continuous learning within the team. **CI/CD Pipeline Management:** * Collaborate on design, maintenance, and improvement of CI/CD pipelines. * Ensure consistent deployment practices across multi\-cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP). * Implement quality gates and automated testing within release pipelines. * Troubleshoot and resolve critical build and release issues. **Infrastructure Automation:** * Champion infrastructure\-as\-code practices (Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM). * Standardize cloud provisioning, configuration, and resource management. * Lead adoption of containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, etc.). * Modernize deployment workflows, phasing out legacy methods. **Development Support:** * Provide tooling and automation to improve developer productivity. * Propose Optimizations for development and staging environments. * Support containerization of applications and services. * Collaborate with engineering teams on deployment requirements and optimization. **Monitoring \& Operations:** * Define monitoring and alerting standards for developer infrastructure and pipelines. * Ensure effective capacity planning and cost optimization. * Participate in high\-priority incident response and post\-mortems. * Maintain clear documentation and operational runbooks **Qualifications** * 6\+ years of experience in DevOps, Platform, or Software Engineering. * 2\+ years of experience providing technical leadership to the DevOps Group. o Expertise with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, etc.). * Strong background in AWS (Azure and GCP exposure a plus). * Proficiency with Docker, Kubernetes, and infrastructure\-as\-code. * Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, or equivalent). * Excellent communication skills for working across global teams. **Education/Certifications/Licenses:** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, Information Systems, or related technical field. * Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered. **Preferred** * Experience with Java and Spring Boot deployments. * Familiarity with database automation, monitoring, and secrets management. * Cloud or Kubernetes certifications (AWS, Azure, CKA/CKAD). **EEO Statement** iCIMS is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at iCIMS. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you’d like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at careers@icims.com. **Compensation And Benefits** iCIMS offers a comprehensive benefits package: medical, vision, and dental, life insurance, pensions, tuition reimbursement, lifestyle spending accounts, and generous paid time off plans (vacation, sick, parental leave). Learn more here: https://careers.icims.com/benefits.

title: Strategy Strategy Analyst
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Strategy Strategy Analyst **About Your Role** Fiserv’s Analyst Program is an early career development program where candidates experience structured assignments within one specific track over a 2 year time period. Each assignment builds upon the other and together will provide a breadth of experience within Financial Tech and unique to you! We are looking for university graduates with drive, and an evolving target for their careers. The best and brightest are the firm’s future, a pipeline of top talent for positions in critical functional areas across the firm. Training curriculum, mentoring, on the job learning, and exposure to senior leadership will be offered during the program, with the opportunity to advance within the firm upon successful completion. Fiserv Analysts will have a target start date of June 2026 depending on your availability. You must possess unrestricted work authorization and not require future sponsorship. As a participant in the Corporate Graduate Programme, you will engage in challenging projects with the potential for a significant impact on the business. This hands\-on experience will not only contribute to the FinTech industry but also enhance the skills necessary for leadership. The program offers a structured training curriculum, mentoring, on\-the\-job learning, and exposure to senior leadership. Successful completion of the two\-year program opens avenues for advancement within our company. * Job Title: 2026 Graduate * Start date: June (2 year programme) * Hours: Monday to Friday 9am\-5\.30pm (37\.5 hours per week) * Location: Dublin (office based) * Salary: €40,000 Please Note: Our Analyst Program interview process begins in February 2026 and typically lasts through April for a June 2026 start date. You can expect to hear from one of our recruiters if your qualifications align. The Graduate Trainee role is designed for recent graduates who are looking to kickstart their careers in the financial services and technology industry. This position offers the opportunity to work alongside experienced professionals and gain valuable insights into the various functions within Fiserv. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the development and implementation of projects. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to support operational goals. * Analyse data and provide actionable insights to enhance decision\-making. * Participate in training programs designed to develop technical and soft skills. * Contribute to process improvement initiatives to enhance client experiences. * Support day\-to\-day operations while learning about the financial services landscape. **Qualifications** * Be a recent graduate holding a Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Business, Information Technology, or a related field. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. * A proactive approach to learning and seeking new challenges. * Ability to work collaboratively in a team\-oriented environment. * Familiarity with technology and an interest in innovation in financial services is a plus. **Perks At Work** * 22 days annual leave, plus bank holidays * Enhanced maternity, paternity, and adoption leave * Healthcare enrolment * Life Insurance \- 4x basic annual salary * Unlimited career development opportunities * Service \& recognition awards * Company laptop **Thank You For Considering Employment With Fiserv. Please** * Apply using your legal name * Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). **Our Commitment To Diversity And Inclusion** Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. **Note To Agencies** Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. **Warning About Fake Job Posts** Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

title: System Development Engineer, ESC
description: **Description** Would you like to be an Engineer that builds the Cloud, rather than an Engineer that just uses it? At AWS, our Engineers look after the behind\-the\-scenes software and tools that make the world's largest cloud computing infrastructure possible. We have an amazing opportunity for you to join a world\-class network team in a dynamic environment that has the feel of a start\-up. As a System Development Engineer you will help to deploy, manage, fix and reinvent the tools, services and components that network engineering rely on to automate our network and keep it operational. Your internal customers are your network engineer colleagues, and through close collaboration, support and exchange of ideas, we share a common goal to serve our external customers and grow through learning and innovation. If you are exceptionally motivated and passionate about the work you do then we welcome you to join us, working in an Agile environment with the most advanced tools, processes and talented people in the industry to make the Cloud a reality. **What You’ll Be Working On** * Simplifying and reinventing systems, processes, and tools to make things better for our customers * Managing and growing innovative, production\-quality tools to solve real operational problems for our network * Investigating technical issues scientifically and thoroughly, and assist in fixing them so they don't come back * Providing technical solutions to real business problems in a global organization **Top Reasons To Join Our Team** * Be a catalyst to deliver truly disruptive products that are growing rapidly * Define, build, own, and run a service in a high growth environment * Build and influence the tools and utilities that are part of the AWS fleet running our internal services * Learning from industry recognized Amazon distinguished engineers Key job responsibilities A SysDE is responsible for maintaining their teams’ services, requiring them to troubleshoot and identify the root causes of any issues that arise within their systems and any subcomponents. A SysDE will utilise testing, monitoring, and validations on their services, tools, and infrastructure to ensure their teams can continuously deploy new versions of the services with minimal interruption. A SysDE will identify areas to invent, refine, and develop automation and tools to reduce manual operations and fulfil the requirements of the business and their customers. **About The Team** The Systems Development team manages all of the software services and platforms that builds, operates, monitors and assures the AWS network within an isolated region. **Basic Qualifications** * Knowledge of fundamentals of networking, security, databases (relational or NoSQL), operating systems (Unix, Linux, and/or Windows) * Experience troubleshooting and debugging technical systems, or experience in managing and troublshooting network and experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities * Experience programming with at least one modern language such as Python, Ruby, Golang, Java, C\+\+, C\#, Rust * This role requires you to be a national of an EU member state. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience of designing, developing or operating cloud services running on AWS or another cloud platform Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Data Services Ireland Limited Job ID: A10401315

title: Systems Engineer/Dev Ops Eng, Managed Operations
description: **Description** AWS has successfully launched the European Sovereign Cloud (ESC), marking a significant development in Utility Computing (UC). description: Talent Acquisition Associate, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope of the role** Based in our new Global Headquarters and reporting to the Talent Acquisition Business Partner, the Talent Acquisition Associate is a key delivery and sourcing role within Mercury’s Agile Talent Acquisition model. You will focus on front‑end recruitment activity, including proactive candidate sourcing, CV screening, and first‑stage interviews. The TA Associate role is key in enabling faster pipelines and higher candidate quality **Location:** Ballycoolin, Dublin 15 (Hybrid: 3 days office / 2 days WFH) **Key Responsibilities** * Proactively source candidates for open roles using multiple sourcing tools including LinkedIn, ATS and other sourcing tools * Build and maintain active and passive talent pipelines aligned to business priorities. * Support high‑volume technical roles through targeted sourcing strategies. * Screen incoming applications and sourced candidates against agreed role criteria. * Create quality shortlists aligned with hiring manager expectations and role priorities * Assist in publishing job advertisements across various platforms * Conduct first‑round interviews and screening calls. * Clearly document interview feedback and recommendations in the ATS. * Partner closely with TA Specialists and TABPs to understand role requirements and priorities. * Provide insights on candidate availability, market trends, and pipeline health. * Assist with recruitment fairs/open days/graduate recruitment fairs where required **Essential Criteria** * 1\-2 years’ experience working in a high volume recruitment environment, either in\-house or agency * Excellent communication and relationship‑building skills * High attention to detail and strong organisational ability * Confident interviewing and questioning skills * Comfortable working with data, systems, and technology * Proactive, curious, and eager to learn * Experience using LinkedIn or similar sourcing tools **Why Join Mercury?** Mercury is committed to building a world‑class talent function that supports our ambitious growth across Europe. title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: Veterinary Nurse – Small Animal All Care Veterinary Centre, Walkinstown, Dublin 12 Full\-time, Permanent Salary €30,000\-€40,000 DOE Grow Your Nursing Career in a Supportive, Friendly Practice Whether you’re a newly qualified Veterinary Nurse finding your feet, or a more experienced nurse ready to settle into a positive, well\-run practice, All Care Veterinary Centre offers a place where you can feel supported, valued, and confident in your role. We’re a well\-established 100% small animal practice in Walkinstown, known for our loyal clients, high standards of care, and genuinely welcoming team. This role is ideal for a junior to mid\-level RVN who enjoys variety, teamwork, and the chance to keep developing without the pressure of out\-of\-hours or on\-call work. A Role Designed Around Balance \& Progression This is a permanent position, with full\-time preferred, though part\-time hours can be considered for the right candidate. A full\-time role is based on a 40\-hour week, with shifts spread across Monday to Saturday. Typical shifts are 8am–4pm or 11am–7pm, offering flexibility across the week. Weekend work is shared fairly, with up to two Saturdays per month (8am–3pm). There’s no out\-of\-hours, no Sundays, and no bank holiday work, allowing you to enjoy a healthy and sustainable work–life balance. Your Day\-to\-Day \- Varied, Hands\-on \& Supported As part of the nursing team at All Care, you’ll be involved across all areas of small animal nursing, including surgical nursing, medical and clinical nursing, inpatient care, dentistry, radiography, laboratory work, infection control, dispensary management, and client communication. You’ll be trusted with real responsibility while always having experienced colleagues around you for support. Whether you’re still building confidence or looking to broaden your skillset, this is a role where you can develop at your own pace and feel genuinely part of the practice. A Modern Practice With a Great Team Culture All Care Veterinary Centre is fully equipped with digital x\-ray, ultrasound, and dental facilities, allowing you to work to a high clinical standard. Our team includes 3 Veterinary Surgeons, 4 Registered Veterinary Nurses, a student nurse, practice manager, and receptionist, creating a close\-knit and supportive environment where everyone plays a key role. We pride ourselves on our positive atmosphere, strong communication, and shared commitment to excellent patient care and client service. Who We’re Looking For We’re open to all levels of experience, from newly qualified nurses to those with several years in practice. You must be VCI registered or eligible to register. What matters most to us is attitude \- someone who is: * A team player with a positive outlook * Caring, organised and motivated * Keen to learn, develop and deliver great patient care Graduate nurses will have access to our Graduate Nurse Academy, while more experienced nurses can explore further development through Nurse Evolve, leadership pathways and funded certificates. Benefits – subject to pro rata We Recognise That Our People Are Fundamental To The Success Of Our Business. Investing In Our Staff, Premises And Processes Is At The Heart Of What We Do. In Addition To a Competitive Salary, You Will Also Benefit From Work life balance * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave Focus on employee wellbeing * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay Development * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities Professional memberships * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Annual pay reviews * Pawesome benefits – various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Relocation packages * Free flu jabs * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurse Committee, Clinical Board and many more) A Practice Where Nurses Feel Supported If you’re looking for a welcoming, well\-run practice where you can grow your skills, enjoy your work, and feel genuinely supported — All Care Veterinary Centre could be the right next step for you. Apply today and join a team that values its nurses at every stage of their career. €30,000 \- €40,000 a year Up to €40,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Warehouse Delivery Operative
description: **About LPI Group** Since our establishment in 2011, Lightning Protection Ireland and Lightning Protection International have delivered cutting\-edge lightning protection systems, earthing systems, and surge protection devices across the European data centre, pharmaceutical, commercial, and renewable energy sectors. In January 2021, to celebrate a successful 10 years in business, we launched our rebrand to LPI Group to reflect our growth as ‘Cloud to Ground Protection’ specialists in high\-demand industries. **Warehouse \& Delivery Operative** **Duties and responsibilities**: * Daily delivery of materials to local construction projects * Prepare, update, and maintain operational documents and records. (Match POD with invoices \& PO) * Manage daily delivery bookings on designated platforms for materials arriving on local and international project sites * Organise and arrange shipments from the Irish headquarters to European project locations when required * Store products in designated locations following stock control procedures * Conduct regular inventory checks and report discrepancies * Operate warehouse equipment such as pallet trucks and forklifts (if certified) * Maintain a clean, organized, and safe working environment * Verify delivery dockets to ensure all items have been received in full and in line with purchase orders * Monitor material delivery dates with suppliers to support project timelines. * Issue purchase orders (POs) using ABM Software or other approved systems when required * Track tools and equipment issued to project teams and arrange calibration certifications as needed (Maintenance of equipment drop off collect, none left without repair) * Prepare material for collection from warehouse manager * Monitor communication groups with site staff to ensure all material requests and project\-related queries are captured and actioned. Reply and confirm time * Supporting logistic manager with outside maintenance * Complete warehouses walk with H\&S and responsible for the H\&S of the warehouse * Supports the warehouse manager with maintenance * Van checks with engineers (Monthly) **Requirements:** * **Forklift licence (essential)** * **Full clean Driving License (essential)** * Previous experience in a warehouse or logistics environment preferred * Good physical fitness and ability to lift heavy items * Strong attention to detail and accuracy * Basic numeracy and literacy skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Forklift licence (essential) * Full Clean Driving License (essential) **Key Skills:** * IT proficient * Time management and organizational skills * Teamwork and communication * Reliability and punctuality * Problem\-solving ability Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €27,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site gym * On\-site parking Work Location: In person
